_comment: Single source of truth for Prism's lens catalog: per-lens weighing-posture descriptions (grouped by axis family) and the --preset task-type lens sets. prism-launch reads this (lens_desc_for, preset_lenses); SKILL.md's Lens Axes table and the suggested-lenses guidance describe it but this file is authoritative. Preset arrays are in canonical tier order (heaviest-reasoning lens first, so it lands on the subagent/gpt slot). The menu is open — add a lens with a one-sentence axis, or a preset of seven names drawn from .lenses. test-prism-launch.sh asserts every preset name is a known lens and every lens.axis is in .axes.

lenses.name: Breadth-Weighted
lenses.desc: weigh wide coverage of options and angles over depth

lenses.name: Depth-Weighted
lenses.desc: weigh deep, thorough analysis of the core over breadth

lenses.name: First-Principles
lenses.desc: weigh how this looks rebuilt from the goal up, ignoring the current shape

lenses.name: Outsider
lenses.desc: weigh how a newcomer from another field would approach it

lenses.name: Reframe
lenses.desc: weigh an alternate prior or problem framing that recasts the question (exploratory, not an attack)

lenses.name: Lateral-Generative
lenses.desc: weigh deliberately non-obvious, out-of-the-box options via analogy, inversion, or constraint removal — novelty over coverage

lenses.name: Empirical
lenses.desc: weigh the claim by what is measurable: baseline, metric, magnitude, base rate — distrust unmeasured assertions

lenses.name: Correctness
lenses.desc: weigh correctness guarantees and edge cases

lenses.name: Causal
lenses.desc: weigh the actual cause-and-effect chain down to the root

lenses.name: Adversarial
lenses.desc: weigh the strongest attacks, flaws, and failure modes — find what breaks

lenses.name: Falsification
lenses.desc: weigh what evidence would prove the leading hypothesis wrong

lenses.name: Disconfirming
lenses.desc: weigh the evidence that most directly contradicts the leading choice

lenses.name: Risk
lenses.desc: weigh what could go wrong and the blast radius if it does

lenses.name: Stakeholder
lenses.desc: weigh who is affected and how: incentives, who bears the cost, second-order effects on the people and systems it touches

lenses.name: Audience
lenses.desc: weigh how the intended audience will actually receive it

lenses.name: Clarity
lenses.desc: weigh how clearly the message lands for its reader

lenses.name: Pragmatist
lenses.desc: weigh the workable path that ships under real constraints — resources, sequencing, what it takes to execute

lenses.name: Simplicity
lenses.desc: weigh the approach with the fewest moving parts

lenses.name: Structural
lenses.desc: weigh the structurally correct fix at the needed scope — reject the expedient hack and scope-padding alike (durable means correct, not bigger)

lenses.name: Temporal
lenses.desc: weigh how the answer changes over time: lifecycle, sequencing, reversibility (one-way vs two-way doors), path dependence
